tripOverview.intro: London in April offers the perfect blend of spring blooms and cultural richness, with mild weather ideal for romantic strolls through historic neighborhoods. The city's incredible diversity, world-class museums, and thriving food scene create an unforgettable backdrop for couples seeking both adventure and intimacy.
tripOverview.vibe: A whirlwind romantic adventure through London's most iconic sights, hidden gems, and incredible vegetarian food scene.

destinationOverview.bestTime: April is excellent with mild weather, fewer crowds, and spring blooms throughout the city's parks

attractions.name: Tower of London
attractions.desc: Historic royal castle housing the Crown Jewels and centuries of fascinating history.

attractions.name: British Museum
attractions.desc: World's largest museum with incredible collections spanning human history and culture.

attractions.name: Westminster Abbey
attractions.desc: Gothic abbey where British monarchs have been crowned for nearly 1000 years.

attractions.name: Tate Modern
attractions.desc: Contemporary art gallery housed in a former power station on the Thames.

attractions.name: Borough Market
attractions.desc: London's oldest food market offering incredible artisanal produce and street food.

destinationOverview.transportation: London's extensive Underground (Tube) system connects all major attractions and neighborhoods efficiently. Black cabs are iconic but expensive, while Uber and Bolt offer convenient rideshare options throughout the city. Walking is often the fastest way for short distances, and the city is very pedestrian-friendly with excellent signage.
destinationOverview.tip: Get an Oyster Card or use contactless payment for seamless tube and bus travel, and always stand right on escalators to let others pass on the left.
destinationOverview.safety: London is very safe for LGBTQ+ travelers with a vibrant community and welcoming atmosphere throughout the city.
destinationOverview.airaloText: Stay connected with affordable eSIM data plans that activate instantly upon arrival in London.

days.intro: Start your London adventure with iconic sights and a romantic Thames-side stroll.

morning.name: Tower of London
morning.desc: Explore the historic fortress and marvel at the Crown Jewels.

morning.name: Tower Bridge
morning.desc: Walk across London's most famous bridge with stunning river views.

afternoon.name: Thames Walk to Borough Market
afternoon.desc: Scenic riverside stroll to London's most famous food market.

afternoon.name: Borough Market Exploration
afternoon.desc: Sample incredible vegetarian street food and artisanal products.

evening.name: Southwark Street Walk
evening.desc: Explore the trendy neighborhood around the market.

evening.name: Dinner at Champor-Champor
evening.desc: Authentic vegetarian Thai cuisine in a cozy candlelit setting.

days.tip: Borough Market is busiest on weekends, so visit on a weekday if possible for a more romantic experience.

days.intro: Dive deep into London's incredible cultural offerings with world-class museums and local cafes.

morning.name: British Museum
morning.desc: Explore ancient civilizations and world cultures in this magnificent museum.

morning.name: Museum Coffee Break
morning.desc: Relax in the beautiful Great Court with coffee and pastries.

afternoon.name: Covent Garden
afternoon.desc: Street performers, boutique shopping, and romantic cobblestone streets.

afternoon.name: National Gallery
afternoon.desc: Masterpieces by Van Gogh, Da Vinci, and other European masters.

evening.name: Soho Exploration
evening.desc: Wander through London's vibrant LGBTQ+ friendly neighborhood.

evening.name: Dinner at Dishoom
evening.desc: Exceptional Indian vegetarian cuisine in a beautiful Bombay cafe setting.

days.tip: Many London museums offer free admission but suggested donations help support their incredible collections.

days.intro: Experience London's green spaces and vibrant cultural scene with parks, galleries, and local flavor.

morning.name: Hyde Park Stroll
morning.desc: Romantic walk through London's most famous park with spring blooms.

morning.name: Kensington Palace Gardens
morning.desc: Beautiful royal gardens and palace with fascinating royal history.

afternoon.name: Notting Hill
afternoon.desc: Colorful houses, vintage shops, and charming neighborhood vibes.

afternoon.name: Portobello Road Market
afternoon.desc: Browse antiques, vintage finds, and unique local crafts.

evening.name: Tate Modern
evening.desc: Contemporary art in a stunning converted power station setting.

evening.name: Dinner at Sketch
evening.desc: Unique vegetarian fine dining in an artistic, Instagram-worthy setting.

days.tip: April is perfect for picnicking in London's parks - grab supplies from local markets for a romantic outdoor meal.

days.intro: Make the most of your final morning with last-minute sights and souvenir shopping.

morning.name: Westminster Abbey
morning.desc: Marvel at Gothic architecture and centuries of royal history.

morning.name: Big Ben & Parliament
morning.desc: Iconic photos at London's most recognizable landmarks.

afternoon.name: Camden Market
afternoon.desc: Alternative shopping, street art, and diverse food vendors.

afternoon.name: Regent's Park
afternoon.desc: Final peaceful stroll through beautiful gardens and rose beds.

evening.name: Airport Transfer
evening.desc: Heathrow Express or Tube to catch your evening flight home.

days.tip: Leave extra time for airport travel and arrive at Heathrow at least 2 hours before international flights.

food.intro: London's vegetarian scene is incredibly diverse, offering everything from traditional British pub fare to innovative international cuisine.
food.tip: Most restaurants offer excellent vegetarian options, and many pubs now have dedicated plant-based menus alongside traditional fare.

neighborhoods.name: Covent Garden
neighborhoods.desc: Central location near theaters, restaurants, and shopping

neighborhoods.name: South Bank
neighborhoods.desc: Thames-side location near Borough Market and Tate Modern
neighborhoods.whyStay: Romantic river views and food scene

neighborhoods.name: Shoreditch
neighborhoods.desc: Trendy East London with great nightlife and street art

luxury.name: The Ned
luxury.vibe: Grand hotel in former banking hall

hotels.tip: Book accommodations near tube stations for easy access to all areas of London, and consider staying south of the river for better value.

getYourGuide.name: Jack the Ripper Walking Tour
getYourGuide.desc: Explore Victorian London's mysteries through historic East End streets.

getYourGuide.name: London Food Walking Tour
getYourGuide.desc: Taste your way through Borough Market and South London's best vegetarian spots.

getYourGuide.name: Thames Sunset Cruise
getYourGuide.desc: Romantic evening cruise past London's illuminated landmarks and bridges.

hiddenGems.name: Neal's Yard
hiddenGems.desc: Colorful courtyard filled with wholefood shops and vegetarian cafes in Covent Garden.

hiddenGems.name: Leake Street Tunnel
hiddenGems.desc: Legal graffiti tunnel under Waterloo Station showcasing incredible street art.

hiddenGems.name: Sky Garden
hiddenGems.desc: Free panoramic city views from London's highest public garden space.

packing.title: April in London
packing.intro: Pack layers for changeable spring weather with occasional rain showers and cool evenings.
packing.luggage: A carry-on suitcase and personal item should be sufficient for a 4-day trip with careful packing.
packing.tip: London weather can change quickly, so always carry a light jacket and umbrella regardless of the morning forecast.

bookTrip.intro: Expect flight costs around $600-800 per person from Philadelphia to London in April 2026.
